lessons.title: 401 Unauthorized — Token Expired or Incorrect
lessons.context: API tests that require authentication must obtain a valid token before each request. Tokens expire and cannot be reused across test sessions. Hardcoding tokens or reusing them from a previous run causes 401 failures that are hard to distinguish from real auth bugs.

problem.cause: Token expired, incorrect credentials, or token not refreshed before test

antipattern.description: Hardcoding or reusing token across tests
antipattern.why_bad: Tokens expire — hardcoded tokens fail silently after expiry, making tests non-deterministic

solution.description: Always obtain a fresh token in beforeEach or beforeAll using a dedicated auth helper.
code: // GOOD — fresh token per test suite

lessons.ai_instruction: Always generate a beforeAll/beforeEach block that calls getAuthToken() and stores the result. Never hardcode tokens in test files.
